workflow.name: Screen and rank CVs with GPT-4o, Google Sheets, Gmail and Telegram
workflow.description:
## How it works
- Candidate submits CV via web form (PDF or DOCX)
- Workflow extracts and prepares CV text automatically
- GPT-4o scores the CV against configurable job requirements
- Ranked shortlist generated — HR notified via Telegram
- Full audit trail saved automatically in Google Sheets

## Set up steps
- Import all 3 workflow JSON files into n8n (10 min)
- Add credentials: OpenAI, Google Sheets, Telegram
- Configure job requirements in the settings node
- Submit a test CV via webhook URL

## Requirements
- n8n instance (cloud or self-hosted)
- OpenAI API key
- Google account
- Telegram bot token
- ## Part of the CV Screening Agent system

- 👉 CV Screening — Comparative Report: https://n8n.io/workflows/15288

parameters.content:
## CV Screening Agent

### How it works

1. The CV Intake Webhook captures incoming CV submissions.
2. Input Validator checks the format and routes it through Format Router.
3. Extract from PDF/DOCX and Fetch Job Requirements prepare text data.
4. LLM Screening Agent screens CVs, followed by JSON Parsing.
5. Duplicate Guard and Score Threshold Gate manage candidate scores and duplicates.
6. Top candidates are sent email invites; others get polite declines.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Configure CV Intake Webhook URL in your application.
- [ ] Set up Google Sheets API credentials for fetching job requirements and logging.
- [ ] Integrate OpenAI API for the LLM Screening Agent.
- [ ] Configure Gmail API credentials for email notifications.
- [ ] Set up Telegram API credentials for HR alerts.

### Customization

Adjust the score threshold in the Score Threshold Gate according to desired candidate quality.

## CV intake and validation

Handle incoming CV data and validate input formats.

## Format identification and extraction

Determine file format and extract content.

## Job requirements fetching and preparation

Fetch job requirements and prepare CV text for screening.

## Screening and JSON parsing

Screen the CV using LLM and parse results to JSON.

## Duplicate checking and scoring

Guard against duplicate entries and assess candidate scores.

## Final logging and communication

Log candidate data and notify HR and candidates via Email and Telegram.

parameters.message: =Dear {{ $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.candidate_name }},  Thank you for your application for the {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.job_title }} position.  We have reviewed your CV and are impressed with your profile. We would like to invite you for an interview to discuss the role further.  Could you share your availability for a 30-minute call this week or next?  Best regards, HR Team
parameters.subject: =Your application for {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.job_title }} — Next steps

parameters.message: =Dear {{ $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.candidate_name }},  Thank you for taking the time to apply for the {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.job_title }} position.  After careful review, we have decided to move forward with other candidates whose experience more closely matches our current needs.  We appreciate your interest and encourage you to apply for future openings.  Best regards, HR Team
parameters.subject: =Your application for {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.job_title }}

values.content:
You are an expert HR screening assistant. You analyze CVs against job requirements and provide structured assessments.

You MUST respond ONLY with valid JSON, no markdown, no extra text.

=Analyze this CV against the job requirements below.

### JOB REQUIREMENTS:
- Position: {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.job_title }}
- Required skills: {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.required_skills }}
- Nice to have: {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.nice_to_have }}
- Minimum experience: {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.min_experience_years }} years
- Description: {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.description }}

### CANDIDATE CV:
{{ $json.cv_text }}

### YOUR TASK:
1. Score the candidate from 0 to 100 based on how well they match the requirements
2. List GREEN FLAGS (strong matches with the job)
3. List RED FLAGS (concerns, gaps, mismatches)
4. Write a brief recommendation (2-3 sentences)

### OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON only, no markdown):
{
  "candidate_name": "",
  "job_id": "{{ $('Receive CV Webhook').item.json.body.job_id }}",
  "score": 0,
  "green_flags": ["flag1", "flag2"],
  "red_flags": ["flag1", "flag2"],
  "skills_matched": ["skill1", "skill2"],
  "skills_missing": ["skill1", "skill2"],
  "experience_years": 0,
  "recommendation": "",
  "tier": ""
}

SCORING GUIDE:
- 90-100: Perfect match, immediate interview
- 70-89: Strong candidate, recommend interview
- 50-69: Partial match, review manually
- 0-49: Poor match, archive

Set "tier" based on the score: "EXCELLENT", "STRONG", "REVIEW", or "ARCHIVE".

parameters.text: =🎯 NEW TOP CANDIDATE!  👤 {{ $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.candidate_name }} 💼 Position: {{ $('Read Job Requirements').item.json.job_title }} 📊 Score: {{ $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.score }}/100 🏆 Tier: {{ $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.tier }}  ✅ Green flags: {{ $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.green_flags.join('\n') }}  ⚠️ Red flags: {{ $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.red_flags.length > 0 ? $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.red_flags.join('\n') : 'None' }}  📝 {{ $('Parse JSON Content').item.json.recommendation }}

Currently building a modular Restaurant AI Suite and publishing ready-to-use workflow templates.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
